untranslatable_reason: The bump-and-run reversal is a complex multi-phase pattern requiring trend line detection and identification of three distinct phases (lead-in, bump, downhill run) that cannot be expressed with point-in-time indicators. The signal requires: (1) detection of an uptrend line, (2) identification of a bump phase above the trend line, and (3) confirmation of breakout below the trend line. Trend line fitting is not available in the allowed indicator set. Volume sequencing across phases also requires multi-bar pattern recognition beyond simple indicator comparisons.
translation_notes: Attempted approximation uses price crossing below SMA-20 (proxy for trend line break) on elevated volume as entry, with reversal exit. However, this significantly oversimplifies the three-phase pattern recognition required by the original signal. A proper implementation would require custom trend line detection logic.
